que: What are the three core steps of layout in Compose?
options: Measure children, decide own size, place children
Load network, save cache, render text

que: When is custom layout most appropriate?
options: When standard layout primitives cannot express the needed measurement or placement behavior
For every button and text label

que: Which Compose API is used to define a custom layout?
What does the measure block receive for child layout?
What important measurement rule does Compose enforce in a normal custom layout?
options: A child should not be measured multiple times with different configurations in the same pass
Every child must be measured three times

que: Why should you prefer standard layouts first?
options: They are simpler and usually sufficient for common UI structure
Standard layouts are required by Firebase

que: What is a common custom layout mistake?
options: Trying to remeasure children repeatedly in one pass

que: What happens after measurement in the layout process?
options: The parent decides size and then places children

que: Why are constraints important in custom layout?
options: They define the size bounds available for measurement

que: Which official docs best support this topic?
options: Custom layouts and layouts in Compose docs
